title: Edgewonk Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of Edgewonk pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Edgewonk Pricing Overview

Edgewonk is a premium trading journal platform designed for serious traders who want to extract actionable insights from their trading data. Unlike many competitors that offer tiered pricing with feature restrictions, Edgewonk takes a different approach: you get the same comprehensive feature set regardless of billing commitment. The platform starts at $169 per month for monthly billing, though annual commitments offer significant savings. The most cost-effective option is a 24-month commitment at $12.38 per month (approximately $297.12 for two years), while a 12-month plan costs $16.42 per month (approximately $197 annually). There is no free tier or trial available, which means you'll need to commit to a paid plan to experience the platform's capabilities.

All Edgewonk Plans Compared

Edgewonk's pricing structure is refreshingly simple: the company offers a single tier with universal access to all features. This means you're not choosing between "Starter," "Professional," and "Enterprise" plans—you're choosing a billing commitment level. Here's the breakdown:

Plan Type Monthly Cost Annual Cost Discount vs. Monthly
Monthly (No Commitment) $169.00 $2,028 —
12-Month Commitment $16.42 ~$197 ~90% savings
24-Month Commitment $12.38 ~$297 (2 years) ~93% savings

Every Edgewonk subscription includes the complete feature suite: advanced trade journaling, detailed performance analytics, broker integrations, backtesting tools, a unique Tiltmeter for emotional trading detection, a trade simulator for "what-if" scenario analysis, and access to educational resources. When you subscribe to Edgewonk, there is no "lite" version or limited-feature tier—you get the full platform immediately.

This all-in approach eliminates decision paralysis about which plan to choose. You're not wondering if you should upgrade to access a critical feature; all features are available from day one. However, it also means that pricing is entirely based on your commitment length, not on the features you need.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Edgewonk does not offer a free tier or a free trial. This is a notable limitation compared to some competitors in the trading journal space. If you want to test the platform's interface, ease of use, or feature set before committing financially, you won't have that opportunity. This is a significant barrier for price-conscious traders or those new to data-driven trading who want to evaluate the platform risk-free.

The lack of a trial means your first experience with Edgewonk requires payment. However, this also signals the company's confidence in its product—they believe the platform delivers enough value to justify an immediate investment. For traders seriously committed to analyzing their trading psychology and performance, the lack of friction from a trial period might be irrelevant. For casual traders testing the waters, it's a genuine drawback.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Edgewonk's pricing is straightforward, and the tool data does not indicate any hidden add-on fees, premium feature charges, or surprise costs. The subscription covers access to all features, broker integrations, and data storage. However, there are a few considerations worth noting:

  • Broker Data Access: While Edgewonk integrates with multiple brokers (supporting forex, commodities, indices, options, stocks, crypto, and futures), some brokers may charge fees for data API access or have restrictions. These are broker costs, not Edgewonk fees, but they're worth verifying with your broker.
  • No Refunds for Annual Commitments: Annual billing is typically non-refundable. If you purchase a 24-month plan and cancel after one year, you won't recover the remaining balance. This is standard practice, but it's important to understand the commitment you're making.
  • Currency Considerations: Edgewonk is headquartered in Vienna, Austria. If you're paying from a currency other than EUR or USD, you may incur foreign exchange fees depending on your payment method and bank.
  • No Add-On Services: The tool data doesn't indicate premium support tiers, white-label options, or enterprise plans, so you're not likely to face upsell pressures beyond the initial subscription.

Edgewonk Pricing vs Competitors

Edgewonk operates in a competitive but specialized space. Most trading journal platforms offer tiered pricing to serve traders at different experience levels. Here's how Edgewonk compares to a few direct competitors (note: competitor pricing is approximate based on typical market offerings, not confirmed data):

Platform Starting Price Annual Cost (Estimated) Free Trial
Edgewonk $169/mo (or $197/yr with commitment) ~$197–$2,028 No
TraderSync (estimated) ~$79–$199/mo ~$950–$2,388 Yes (7 days)
Edgecase (estimated) ~$99–$249/mo ~$1,188–$2,988 Yes (14 days)
Trademetria (estimated) ~$59–$159/mo ~$708–$1,908 Yes (Free tier available)

Value Proposition: Edgewonk's positioning is interesting. With annual commitment, it's competitively priced at roughly $197 per year, making it cheaper than most tiered competitors on a per-month basis. The key trade-off is the lack of flexibility: you commit to annual billing and have no free trial to evaluate first. Competitors like TraderSync and Edgecase offer shorter commitment periods and trial access, making them lower-risk choices for newcomers. However, if you're committed to using a trading journal long-term, Edgewonk's all-in-one pricing is attractive.

Is Edgewonk Worth the Price?

Best For: Edgewonk is worth the price for analytical traders, forex traders, and anyone serious about improving trading psychology. The platform's Tiltmeter (emotional trading detection) and trade simulator are genuinely unique features that justify the premium. If you're a trader who wants statistical edge analysis without feature limitations, Edgewonk delivers value.

Not Worth It If: You're a casual trader testing your first strategy, a beginner unwilling to commit financially without a trial, or someone who trades only one asset class and doesn't need the breadth of integration support (forex, crypto, futures, stocks, options, commodities, indices).

Upgrade Considerations: Since all features are available at every tier, there's no "upgrading" in the traditional sense. Your choice is purely about commitment length. If you know you'll actively journal trades for at least 12 months, the annual commitment is an obvious choice—you'll save ~90% compared to monthly billing. The 24-month option is best for traders who are confident they'll use the platform for two years.

How to Save on Edgewonk

Edgewonk's discount structure is straightforward and based purely on commitment length:

  • Annual Commitment: Commit to 12 months and pay $16.42/month (~$197 annually) instead of $169/month. This saves approximately 90% versus month-to-month billing.
  • Two-Year Commitment: For traders confident in long-term platform use, the 24-month option at $12.38/month (~$148.56/year) is the lowest per-month cost, saving approximately 93% versus monthly billing.
  • No Promotional Discounts: The tool data does not indicate student discounts, seasonal promotions, or referral programs. Edgewonk's pricing appears fixed, with no additional savings mechanisms beyond the standard annual commitment tiers.
  • Broker Bundling: Some brokers occasionally include trading journal subscriptions as part of their services. If your broker partners with Edgewonk, there may be bundled discounts available—worth checking with your broker directly.

Bottom Line: If you're serious about becoming a data-driven trader, Edgewonk's annual commitment at ~$197 per year is reasonable for a fully-featured platform. The lack of a trial is a barrier, but it reflects Edgewonk's confidence in its product. For traders ready to commit and analyze their edge with professional-grade tools, Edgewonk represents solid long-term value. Compare this decision carefully to free alternatives and trial-based competitors before committing to a two-year plan.

For a detailed review of Edgewonk's features and user feedback, visit our Edgewonk review page. To compare Edgewonk with other trading journal platforms side-by-side, check out our trading journal comparison page.
